hyperedges.description: CLI uses Command pattern with executor, writer, and launch command types

hyperedges.name: Visitor Pattern
hyperedges.description: Script executor uses visitor-like pattern to dispatch commands via type switch

hyperedges.name: Facade Pattern
hyperedges.description: Script package provides simplified facade over parsing and execution

hyperedges.name: Manager Pattern
hyperedges.description: OutputManager coordinates file operations with mutex protection

hyperedges.name: Handler Pattern
hyperedges.description: Server handler implements RPC handler interface for request dispatch

hyperedges.name: Factory Pattern
hyperedges.description: Constructor functions create properly initialized instances

hyperedges.name: Strategy Pattern
hyperedges.description: Executor uses strategy pattern with injectable function handlers

hyperedges.description: Proxy detection and routing layer for CLI commands

hyperedges.name: autoui/internal
hyperedges.description: Internal implementation for autoui with reflection, widget state, and custom data extraction

hyperedges.name: E2E Tests
hyperedges.description: End-to-end test suite covering launch, crash diagnostics, output capture, and RPC

hyperedges.name: Documentation
hyperedges.description: Project documentation including README, integration guide, and CLI reference

hyperedges.name: Integration Methods
hyperedges.description: Two integration methods: Patch (zero-code) and Library (direct API)

hyperedges.name: Automation Features
hyperedges.description: Core automation capabilities: input injection, screenshots, scripts, custom commands

hyperedges.name: Crash Diagnostic System
hyperedges.description: System for capturing and reporting crash info via launch socket and proxy
autoui component architecture: CLI/User → autoebiten → autoui → Tree Walker/Finder/Caller → ebitenui
Integration method decision: Patch (no code changes) vs Library (direct API)
Testing mode decision: Black-box (Launch) vs White-box (Mock)
autoui command suite for UI automation: tree, at, find, xpath, exists, call, highlight
Crash diagnostics capture flow: launch proxy captures output → log_diff and proxy_error displayed on failure
E2E testing pattern: autoui.tree → autoui.find → autoui.call → screenshot verification
Custom data extraction rules: struct → xml tags, map → attributes, primitives → custom_data attr
Widget automation without coordinates: tree discovery → identify by attributes → method invocation

nodes.label: autoui RadioGroup & TabBook Support Implementation Plan

attributes.goal: Extend autoui to support RadioGroup and TabBook widget automation
attributes.architecture: RadioGroup requires user registration; TabBook uses tree traversal with reflection

nodes.norm_label: autoui radiogroup & tabbook support implementation plan

attributes.goal: Add autoui.exists command for widget waiting with wait-for
Add integrate.AfterDraw() to handle post-draw operations without import cycles

attributes.bugs: XPath matching, race conditions, orphan widgets, slice support, button text

attributes.goal: Automate documentation examples with real runs and output capture

nodes.label: Launch Socket for Crash Diagnostics Design

attributes.problem: No visibility into crash scenarios when game launched in background
attributes.solution: Single launch socket persisting throughout launch lifecycle

nodes.norm_label: launch socket for crash diagnostics design

attributes.key_features: Inline config, direct command execution, Go code extraction

attributes.problem: E2E tests need to wait for widgets to appear dynamically
attributes.solution: autoui.exists command returning JSON for wait-for compatibility
